description: This pipeline appears to be designed for processing and analyzing biomedical images, specifically focusing on filtering, enhancing, and annotating image data based on various criteria such as annotations and metadata. It begins by importing images and then applies a series of operations including contrast enhancement, color channel splitting, and setting look-up tables from predefined color maps. The pipeline also involves adding annotations to the images based on their properties and filtering them according to specific annotation values. Additionally, it converts image formats and extracts metadata, ultimately producing annotated and processed images as output.

a4c3f0bf-432f-4756-a3fe-a8bd59413317.name: annotate-filter-by-annotation
a4c3f0bf-432f-4756-a3fe-a8bd59413317.description: Filters data based on the annotation value.

0660eee7-ee6a-4e33-8768-3565de92ee6f.description: Extracts the OME metadata XML from an OME image

f2f41da7-5caf-4a7e-8e85-aa8dd4dc8c01.name: ij1-contrast-histogram-enhancer
f2f41da7-5caf-4a7e-8e85-aa8dd4dc8c01.description: Implementation of the ImageJ contrast enhancer feature that uses histograms and normalization methods.

15c7badc-68f6-4700-b859-64d6d4f39f07.name: ij1-color-split-channels-2
15c7badc-68f6-4700-b859-64d6d4f39f07.description: Splits multichannel images into multiple greyscale images. This operation is applied for each 2D image slice.

54df5175-5291-46e1-b1b2-9e72807ef878.description: Sets the LUT of the image from a predefined color map. This does not change the pixel data.
